sentence: El sielo no esta klaro oy.
ipa: el sielo no esta klaɾo oj
gloss_en: The sky is not clear today.
notes: Aki Yerushalayim spelling; ⟨ie⟩ 'sielo' rendered as hiatus [ie] not glide [je] by the engine (attested [ˈsjelo]) — see PR body; base per penny2002.

gloss_en: These shoes cost forty liras.
notes: Ottoman-Turkish currency loan 'liras', seseo lexicalised in spelling ⟨s⟩ per harris1994; number frame per schmid2013.

sentence: La sita es amanyana a las tres.
ipa: la sita es amaɲana a las tɾes
gloss_en: The appointment is tomorrow at three.
notes: Digraph ⟨ny⟩>[ɲ] 'amanyana' per harris1994; junction 'sita es' per schmid2013.

gloss_en: My family lives in Istanbul.
notes: Yeismo variant ⟨y⟩>[j] 'famiya', betacism ⟨b⟩=⟨v⟩ 'bive', Turkish toponym 'Estambol' per harris1994; contact loans per schmid2013.

gloss_en: Go straight to the street.
notes: Imperative 'va', ⟨ch⟩>[tʃ] 'derecho', ⟨y⟩>[j] 'kaye' per harris1994; base per penny2002.

gloss_en: This bread is very good.
notes: ⟨ue⟩ 'bueno' realised as hiatus [ue] by the engine (attested glide [ˈbweno]) — see PR body; junction 'es muy' per penny2002.

gloss_en: Where is the market?
notes: Turkish loan 'charshi' (market) with ⟨ch⟩>[tʃ] and ⟨sh⟩>[ʃ] per harris1994; Ottoman lexicon per schmid2013.

gloss_en: How old are you?
notes: Palatal ⟨ny⟩>[ɲ] 'anyos'; ⟨ie⟩ 'tienes' realised [ie] hiatus (see PR body) per harris1994; base per penny2002.

sentence: Si yueve, mos kedamos en kaza.
ipa: si jueve mos keðamos en kaza
gloss_en: If it rains, we stay at home.
notes: Voiced ⟨z⟩>[z] 'kaza' (the preserved /z/, not seseo which is spelled ⟨s⟩) per harris1994; sibilant retention per schmid2013.

gloss_en: Yesterday I bought bread and cheese.
notes: Voiced ⟨z⟩>[z] 'kezo' (Ladino 'kezo' < queso) per harris1994; /z/ maintained per penny2002.

gloss_en: Tomorrow we are going to travel.
notes: Voiced ⟨j⟩>[ʒ] 'viajar' (never merged to [x]), ⟨ny⟩>[ɲ] 'amanyana' per harris1994; the diagnostic /ʒ/ per schmid2013.

gloss_en: Call me later.
notes: Imperative + enclitic 'yamame' with ⟨y⟩>[j] per harris1994; base per penny2002.

gloss_en: Good day, how are you?
notes: Greeting pair; ⟨ue⟩ 'buenos' hiatus by engine (see PR body); junction 'komo estas' per penny2002.

gloss_en: He who talks a lot does little.
notes: Proverb; F->[∅] 'avla' (< habla) and 'aze' (< hace) with intervocalic ⟨s⟩ not present — voiced context per harris1994; F-history per penny2002.

gloss_en: The woman sings a song.
notes: Voiced ⟨j⟩>[ʒ] 'mujer' per harris1994; elision 'kanta una' per schmid2013.

sentence: El gato durme enriva de la silla.
ipa: el ɡato duɾme enɾiva de la siʎa
gloss_en: The cat sleeps on the chair.
notes: Conservative palatal lateral ⟨ll⟩>[ʎ] 'silla' (no yeismo) per harris1994; /ʎ/ retention per schmid2013.

gloss_en: We ate fish with rice.
notes: ⟨sh⟩>[ʃ] 'peshkado', voiced ⟨z⟩>[z] 'arroz' with geminate rr>trill [r] per harris1994; sibilants per schmid2013.

sentence: El perro korre por la guerta.
ipa: el pero kore poɾ la ɡueɾta
gloss_en: The dog runs to the orchard.
notes: Geminate rr>trill [r] 'perro'/'korre' per harris1994; ⟨ue⟩ 'guerta' hiatus by engine (see PR body) per penny2002.

gloss_en: I brought fresh flowers from the market.
notes: ⟨sh⟩>[ʃ] 'trushe'/'charshi', preserved initial F- 'flores'/'freskas' per harris1994; F-preservation per penny2002.

sentence: El djoven melda un livro en kaza.
ipa: el dʒoven melda un livɾo en kaza
gloss_en: The young man reads a book at home.
notes: Affricate ⟨dj⟩>[dʒ] 'djoven', Hebrew-origin 'melda' (reads/studies), voiced ⟨z⟩>[z] 'kaza' per harris1994; loan strata per schmid2013.
